ticket.subject: USPS Flat Rate Box Dimensions & Packing Behavior Issues
ticket.raw_subject: USPS Flat Rate Box Dimensions & Packing Behavior Issues
ticket.description:
Hi there,

First off, I’m very happy I purchased the plugin — it’s going to be a big help on my site. However, I’ve run into a few issues with the USPS flat rate box setup and packing logic that I wanted to bring to your attention.

1. Missing and incorrect USPS Flat Rate box sizes

Right now, the plugin only lists the side-loading USPS Medium Flat Rate box. There is also a top-loading USPS Medium Flat Rate box with the following dimensions:

*

Medium Flat Rate Top Loader

**

ID: 11 x 8.5 x 5.5

**

OD: 11.25 x 8.75 x 6

Because this top-loading medium box isn’t available, the plugin is skipping it and jumping straight to the Large Flat Rate box, which overcharges the customer and uses a larger box than needed.

In addition, the dimensions for the Large Flat Rate box in the plugin are not accurate. You currently have it listed as 12 x 12 x 5.5 for both inside and outside dimensions. The actual USPS dimensions are:

*

Large Flat Rate Top Loader

**

ID: 12 x 11.75 x 8

**

OD: 12.25 x 12 x 8.5

The plugin’s current dimensions effectively lose about 3 inches of usable height.

There is also a Large Flat Rate Side Loading box that is not present in the plugin:

*

Large Flat Rate Side Loader

**

ID: 14.875 x 11.875 x 3

**

OD: 15 x 12 x 3.125

All of the above USPS flat rate boxes are rated for 70 lbs domestic, and many of us rely on these free USPS-supplied boxes rather than our own custom packaging. Having all of these boxes available, with accurate dimensions, is important for correct packing and pricing.

2. Behavior when using only USPS Flat Rate boxes

For stores that exclusively use USPS Flat Rate boxes (and not their own custom boxes), the plugin tends to jump to the Large Flat Rate box instead of selecting the smallest appropriate flat rate option. In my case, Priority Mail will sometimes show up using a custom box that is cheaper than a USPS Flat Rate box, but many businesses don’t buy their own boxes and rely solely on USPS flat rate packaging.

3. Product dimensions being changed and forcing the wrong box

I’ve attached screenshots for this part.

The product I’m testing is:

*

Weight: 0.65 lb

*

Dimensions: 10 x 8 x 5

This should easily fit into a Medium Top-Loading Flat Rate box. However, the plugin is changing the item’s dimensions to 12 x 5.5 x 12 during packing, which forces it into a Large Flat Rate box instead of the correct medium box.

In my own setup, I created a custom top-loading medium box, and when that is used, Ground Advantage and Priority Mail (in my own box) show up correctly. But because the USPS box dimensions in the plugin are incorrect and incomplete, the system keeps selecting the highest-priced Large Flat Rate box instead of a Medium Flat Rate box supplied by USPS.

Thank you for your time and help with this situation. If you need any additional screenshots, test data, or examples from my store setup, I’m happy to provide them.
--
All the best,

Kenny Conklin

CEO Hobby Link International Inc.

Editor Hobby Link International Publications

https://www.hobbylinkinternational.com
https://www.community.hobbylinkinternational.com

Hobby Link International Inc.
2 Oakwood Drive
Parlin NJ 08859

1-347-993-6806

comments.body:
Hi there,

First off, I’m very happy I purchased the plugin — it’s going to be a big help on my site. However, I’ve run into a few issues with the USPS flat rate box setup and packing logic that I wanted to bring to your attention.

**1. Missing and incorrect USPS Flat Rate box sizes**

Right now, the plugin only lists the _side-loading_ USPS Medium Flat Rate box. There is also a _top-loading_ USPS Medium Flat Rate box with the following dimensions:

-

**Medium Flat Rate Top Loader**

  -

ID: 11 x 8.5 x 5.5

  -

OD: 11.25 x 8.75 x 6

Because this top-loading medium box isn’t available, the plugin is skipping it and jumping straight to the **Large Flat Rate box**, which overcharges the customer and uses a larger box than needed.

In addition, the dimensions for the Large Flat Rate box in the plugin are not accurate. You currently have it listed as 12 x 12 x 5.5 for both inside and outside dimensions. The actual USPS dimensions are:

-

**Large Flat Rate Top Loader**

  -

ID: 12 x 11.75 x 8

  -

OD: 12.25 x 12 x 8.5

The plugin’s current dimensions effectively lose about 3 inches of usable height.

There is also a **Large Flat Rate Side Loading** box that is not present in the plugin:

-

**Large Flat Rate Side Loader**

  -

ID: 14.875 x 11.875 x 3

  -

OD: 15 x 12 x 3.125

All of the above USPS flat rate boxes are rated for 70 lbs domestic, and many of us rely on these free USPS-supplied boxes rather than our own custom packaging. Having all of these boxes available, with accurate dimensions, is important for correct packing and pricing.

**2. Behavior when using only USPS Flat Rate boxes**

For stores that exclusively use USPS Flat Rate boxes (and not their own custom boxes), the plugin tends to jump to the **Large Flat Rate box** instead of selecting the smallest appropriate flat rate option. In my case, Priority Mail will sometimes show up using a custom box that is cheaper than a USPS Flat Rate box, but many businesses don’t buy their own boxes and rely solely on USPS flat rate packaging.

**3. Product dimensions being changed and forcing the wrong box**

I’ve attached screenshots for this part.

The product I’m testing is:

-

**Weight:** 0.65 lb

-

**Dimensions:** 10 x 8 x 5

This should easily fit into a **Medium Top-Loading Flat Rate box**. However, the plugin is changing the item’s dimensions to **12 x 5.5 x 12** during packing, which forces it into a **Large Flat Rate box** instead of the correct medium box.

In my own setup, I created a custom top-loading medium box, and when that is used, Ground Advantage and Priority Mail (in my own box) show up correctly. But because the USPS box dimensions in the plugin are incorrect and incomplete, the system keeps selecting the highest-priced Large Flat Rate box instead of a Medium Flat Rate box supplied by USPS.

Thank you for your time and help with this situation. If you need any additional screenshots, test data, or examples from my store setup, I’m happy to provide them.
--

All the best,


Kenny Conklin



CEO Hobby Link International Inc.



Editor Hobby Link International Publications



https://www.hobbylinkinternational.com

https://www.community.hobbylinkinternational.com



Hobby Link International Inc.

2 Oakwood Drive

Parlin NJ 08859



1-347-993-6806

1. Missing and incorrect USPS Flat Rate box sizes

Right now, the plugin only lists the side-loading USPS Medium Flat Rate box. There is also a top-loading USPS Medium Flat Rate box with the following dimensions:

  • Medium Flat Rate Top Loader

    • ID: 11 x 8.5 x 5.5

    • OD: 11.25 x 8.75 x 6

Because this top-loading medium box isn’t available, the plugin is skipping it and jumping straight to the Large Flat Rate box, which overcharges the customer and uses a larger box than needed.

In addition, the dimensions for the Large Flat Rate box in the plugin are not accurate. You currently have it listed as 12 x 12 x 5.5 for both inside and outside dimensions. The actual USPS dimensions are:

  • Large Flat Rate Top Loader

    • ID: 12 x 11.75 x 8

    • OD: 12.25 x 12 x 8.5

The plugin’s current dimensions effectively lose about 3 inches of usable height.

There is also a Large Flat Rate Side Loading box that is not present in the plugin:

  • Large Flat Rate Side Loader

    • ID: 14.875 x 11.875 x 3

    • OD: 15 x 12 x 3.125

All of the above USPS flat rate boxes are rated for 70 lbs domestic, and many of us rely on these free USPS-supplied boxes rather than our own custom packaging. Having all of these boxes available, with accurate dimensions, is important for correct packing and pricing.

2. Behavior when using only USPS Flat Rate boxes

For stores that exclusively use USPS Flat Rate boxes (and not their own custom boxes), the plugin tends to jump to the Large Flat Rate box instead of selecting the smallest appropriate flat rate option. In my case, Priority Mail will sometimes show up using a custom box that is cheaper than a USPS Flat Rate box, but many businesses don’t buy their own boxes and rely solely on USPS flat rate packaging.

3. Product dimensions being changed and forcing the wrong box

I’ve attached screenshots for this part.

The product I’m testing is:

This should easily fit into a Medium Top-Loading Flat Rate box. However, the plugin is changing the item’s dimensions to 12 x 5.5 x 12 during packing, which forces it into a Large Flat Rate box instead of the correct medium box.

In my own setup, I created a custom top-loading medium box, and when that is used, Ground Advantage and Priority Mail (in my own box) show up correctly. But because the USPS box dimensions in the plugin are incorrect and incomplete, the system keeps selecting the highest-priced Large Flat Rate box instead of a Medium Flat Rate box supplied by USPS.

comments.body:
Hello Kenny,

Thank you for reaching out with your case.

We will be happy to look into the USPS Flat rate box sizes case you have shared.

To verify the same from our end, could you please share the official USPS page link where these box sizes are listed.

We will check and verify the case and take necessary action as needed.

Awaiting your response.

Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
Hi Touseef,

Thank you for the quick response.

USPS does not provide a single consolidated table with the internal and external dimensions of all their Flat Rate boxes, and several of the box types do not have dedicated USPS web pages with ID/OD measurements. Because of this, the most accurate and reliable source is the **physical USPS packaging itself**, where the official dimensions are printed directly on each flat, unassembled box.

I’ve taken photos of all the USPS Flat Rate boxes I use in my business and have attached them to this email. Each set includes:

1.

A photo of the box as it comes from USPS

2.

A close-up photo of the printed ID/OD dimensions

USPS clearly labels every box except for **the Large Flat Rate Side-Loading box**. USPS does not label the Large Flat Rate Side-Loading box with the words “Large Flat Rate,” but the printed ID/OD measurements confirm it is the correct official Flat Rate Side Loader.

Attached are photos for the following box types:

-

Small Flat Rate Box

-

Medium Flat Rate Box – Top Loading

-

Medium Flat Rate Box – Side Loading

-

Large Flat Rate Box – Top Loading

-

Large Flat Rate Box – Side Loading (unlabeled, but official)

Please let me know if you need any additional photos, measurements, or information.

Thank you again for your help,
Kenny

 ![](https://ssl.gstatic.com/docs/doclist/images/icon_10_generic_list.png) USPS_Large_FlatRate_SideLoad_Dimensions.jpg.jpg

 ![](https://ssl.gstatic.com/docs/doclist/images/icon_10_generic_list.png) USPS_Large_FlatRate_SideLoad_Full.jpg.jpg

 ![](https://ssl.gstatic.com/docs/doclist/images/icon_10_generic_list.png) USPS_Large_FlatRate_TopLoad_Dimensions.jpg.jpg

 ![](https://ssl.gstatic.com/docs/doclist/images/icon_10_generic_list.png) USPS_Large_FlatRate_TopLoad_Full.jpg.jpg

 ![](https://ssl.gstatic.com/docs/doclist/images/icon_10_generic_list.png) USPS_Medium_FlatRate_SideLoad_Dimensions.jpg.jpg

 ![](https://ssl.gstatic.com/docs/doclist/images/icon_10_generic_list.png) USPS_Medium_FlatRate_SideLoad_Full.jpg.jpg

 ![](https://ssl.gstatic.com/docs/doclist/images/icon_10_generic_list.png) USPS_Medium_FlatRate_TopLoad_Dimensions.jpg.jpg

 ![](https://ssl.gstatic.com/docs/doclist/images/icon_10_generic_list.png) USPS_Medium_FlatRate_TopLoad_Full.jpg.jpg

 ![](https://ssl.gstatic.com/docs/doclist/images/icon_10_generic_list.png) USPS_Small_FlatRate_Box_Dimensions.jpg.jpg

 ![](https://ssl.gstatic.com/docs/doclist/images/icon_10_generic_list.png) USPS_Small_FlatRate_Box_Full.jpg.jpg

comments.body:
Hello Kenny,

Thank you for your response.

I do acknowledge the missing box sizes and the dimensions mismatch case you have shared.

However, for us to make changes in the app, we will have to verify the same details on USPS official sources which is the reason for my request to share any official page where these details are listed.

This will help us in reaching out to USPS team to get confirmation with reference and make changes in the plugin if necessary.

Could you please refer to this page - https://store.usps.com/store/shipping-supplies and let us know if all the boxes listed here are correct and as per the details you are sharing?

Once confirmed, we will check your case further.

Awaiting your response.

Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
H

Hi Touseef,

Thank you for your quick reply.

The USPS page you linked does list the correct Flat Rate box types, and the boxes shown there match the same physical boxes I photographed. However, that USPS page does not provide any ID/OD (internal or external) dimensions for the Flat Rate boxes. USPS does not publish the exact dimensions on their store site, which is why I provided photos of the physical boxes with the printed measurements directly from USPS packaging.

To answer your question:

Yes, the USPS store page shows the correct box types.
No, it does not include the dimensions needed to verify or correct the plugin.

This is why the printed dimensions on the actual USPS packaging are the most accurate reference available. These measurements come directly from USPS manufacturing and are printed on each box they supply.

Please let me know if you need me to identify which photo corresponds to which box type shown on the USPS page. I am happy to provide any additional details.

Thank you again,
Kenny

Small Flat Rate Priority Box

(You have this one in the plugin. This is the commonly used small box.)
https://store.usps.com/store/product/priority-mail-flat-rate-small-box-P_SMALL_FRB

Small Flat Rate Priority Box – Type 2
(Currently not included in the plugin. This version offers slightly more internal space at the same small flat rate price.)
https://store.usps.com/store/product/priority-mail-small-box-P_O_1096L

Medium Flat Rate Priority Box – Top Loading
(This is an important one missing from the plugin. Without this medium top-loading box, packing tends to skip directly to the large flat rate box. USPS uses this box for both domestic and international service. You do have the this box but it is only set to international)
https://store.usps.com/store/product/priority-mail-flat-rate-medium-box-P_O_FRB1

Medium Flat Rate Priority Box – Side Loading
(This one is already included in the plugin.)
https://store.usps.com/store/product/priority-mail-flat-rate-side-loading-medium-box-P_O_FRB2

Large Flat Rate Priority Box – Top Loading
(This large flat rate box is included in the plugin.)
https://store.usps.com/store/product/priority-mail-flat-rate-large-box-P_LARGE_FRB

Large Flat Rate Priority Box – Top Loading Type 2
(This version is not currently in the plugin and offers slightly different usable space at the same large flat rate price.)
https://store.usps.com/store/product/priority-mail-large-box-P_O_BOX7

Large Flat Rate Priority Box – Side Loading
(This large side-loading flat rate box is not currently included in the plugin.)
https://store.usps.com/store/product/priority-mail-side-loading-large-box-P_O_1095

 This is why the printed dimensions on the actual USPS packaging are the most accurate reference available. These measurements come directly from USPS manufacturing and are printed on each box they supply. 
 Please let me know if you need me to identify which photo corresponds to which box type shown on the USPS page. I am happy to provide any additional details. 
 Thank you again,

Kenny 

Small Flat Rate Priority Box     
(You have this one in the plugin. This is the commonly used small box.)

https://store.usps.com/store/product/priority-mail-flat-rate-small-box-P_SMALL_FRB
 Small Flat Rate Priority Box – Type 2

(Currently not included in the plugin. This version offers slightly more internal space at the same small flat rate price.)

https://store.usps.com/store/product/priority-mail-small-box-P_O_1096L Medium Flat Rate Priority Box – Top Loading

(This is an important one missing from the plugin. Without this medium top-loading box, packing tends to skip directly to the large flat rate box. USPS uses this box for both domestic and international service. You do have the this box but it is only set to international)

https://store.usps.com/store/product/priority-mail-flat-rate-medium-box-P_O_FRB1

Medium Flat Rate Priority Box – Side Loading

(This one is already included in the plugin.)

https://store.usps.com/store/product/priority-mail-flat-rate-side-loading-medium-box-P_O_FRB2

Large Flat Rate Priority Box – Top Loading

(This large flat rate box is included in the plugin.)

https://store.usps.com/store/product/priority-mail-flat-rate-large-box-P_LARGE_FRB

Large Flat Rate Priority Box – Top Loading Type 2

(This version is not currently in the plugin and offers slightly different usable space at the same large flat rate price.)

https://store.usps.com/store/product/priority-mail-large-box-P_O_BOX7

Large Flat Rate Priority Box – Side Loading

(This large side-loading flat rate box is not currently included in the plugin.)

https://store.usps.com/store/product/priority-mail-side-loading-large-box-P_O_1095

comments.body:
Hey guys I have been trying to get a reset code for my password on the main site so I can update my credit card info but I am not getting a reset email at all :( any help would be appreciated.

I saw an update a little while back and checked the shipping boxes I am assuming the fixed shipping boxes wasn't in that update?

comments.plain_body:
Hey guys I have been trying to get a reset code for my password on the main site so I can update my credit card info but I am not getting a reset email at all :( any help would be appreciated.
 I saw an update a little while back and checked the shipping boxes I am assuming the fixed shipping boxes wasn't in that update?

comments.body:
Hello Kenny,

You are currently using the **WooCommerce Shipping Services** plugin, which is an advanced solution. The subscription and payment details for this plugin are managed directly within the plugin itself, and there is no need to create a separate PluginHive account.

To update your card details, you will need to subscribe to the plugin again using the new card information. You can do this by navigating to **Plugin → Accounts → Manage Subscription → Change Subscription**, where you can select a plan and complete the payment. Once the payment is successful, the subscription will be activated with the new card details, and future renewals will be processed using the updated payment method. (Screenshots attached for reference.)

For your reference, the current subscription is scheduled to expire on **January 13, 2025**. You may update the subscription using the new card details one or two days prior to the expiry date to ensure uninterrupted service.

Regarding the missing USPS Flat Rate boxes, we are actively working on this enhancement. The additional box options will be included in an upcoming release, which is expected to be available by the end of January 2025. Rest assured, we will keep you updated on progress.

Please feel free to reach out to us anytime if you need assistance from our end.

Thanks and Regards,
Manohar V
Team PluginHive

 ![](https://pluginhive.zendesk.com/attachments/token/e5OpdBjdKX0Q3Qzu9Ml2LERSF/?name=image.png)
comments.html_body:
Hello Kenny,
 
You are currently using the WooCommerce Shipping Services plugin, which is an advanced solution. The subscription and payment details for this plugin are managed directly within the plugin itself, and there is no need to create a separate PluginHive account.
 
To update your card details, you will need to subscribe to the plugin again using the new card information. You can do this by navigating to Plugin → Accounts → Manage Subscription → Change Subscription, where you can select a plan and complete the payment. Once the payment is successful, the subscription will be activated with the new card details, and future renewals will be processed using the updated payment method. (Screenshots attached for reference.)
 
For your reference, the current subscription is scheduled to expire on January 13, 2025. You may update the subscription using the new card details one or two days prior to the expiry date to ensure uninterrupted service.
 
Regarding the missing USPS Flat Rate boxes, we are actively working on this enhancement. The additional box options will be included in an upcoming release, which is expected to be available by the end of January 2025. Rest assured, we will keep you updated on progress.
 
Please feel free to reach out to us anytime if you need assistance from our end.
 
Thanks and Regards,
Manohar V
Team PluginHive
